message:
test(fcp-conformance/replay-window): pin ReplayWindow per-frame sliding-bitmap protection (br-j2jz6)

ReplayWindow tracks accepted FCPS frame sequence numbers via a 128-bit
sliding bitmap. It fires BEFORE expensive MAC verification so an
attacker replaying captured frames cannot force the receiver into a
CPU-exhaustion loop. Distinct from HelloReplayWindow (handshake nonce,
already pinned in session_nonce_replay_conformance.rs) which keys on
(from_node, nonce). ReplayWindow keys on seq number alone within an
already-authenticated session and had zero conformance coverage.

Adds 10 tests against the public fcp_protocol::session::ReplayWindow
API:

- seq_zero_is_always_rejected (unset sentinel — closes the inject-0
  bypass)
- strictly_increasing_seq_is_always_accepted
- replay_of_same_seq_is_rejected_on_second_presentation
- out_of_window_seq_is_rejected
- out_of_order_in_window_seq_is_still_accepted (non-strict ordering;
  the bitmap remembers each seq independently)
- check_is_non_mutating (probe-then-decide caller pattern stays
  correct; check() does NOT consume the seq slot)
- far_future_seq_slides_the_window_and_evicts_older_entries
- bitmap_caps_at_128_entries_regardless_of_window_size (the u128
  width is the hard ceiling regardless of configured window_size)
- highest_seq_advances_only_on_acceptance (out-of-order or rejected
  presentations MUST NOT advance the high-water mark)
- window_size_zero_is_clamped_to_at_least_one (misconfigured zero
  still rejects exact replays)

All 10 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
session_replay_window_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
